text: Performing strides with a weighted vest (20% body mass) during warm-up improves peak running speed by approximately 2.9% compared to unweighted strides.
Weighted-vest strides during warm-up improve running economy by approximately 6.0% in subsequent running efforts.
Weighted-vest strides during warm-up increase leg stiffness by approximately 20.4%, which mediates the improvements in both running economy and performance.

A warm-up protocol of 10-min jog, 5-min submaximal run, and six 10-second strides with a weighted vest, followed by 10-min recovery, produces a priming effect that enhances subsequent endurance performance.
Changes in leg stiffness from weighted-vest warm-up can explain all the observed improvements in peak running speed and running economy.
Weighted-vest strides during warm-up produce small-to-moderate reductions in cardiorespiratory cost during subsequent running.
